reasoning: The threshold for this question is more than 10 times the recent average. Since the most recent 360-day average plus one is 1.000, 11 or more riots are required in a 30-day window to resolve positive. Micronesia (Federated States of) is an exceptionally peaceful country with zero recorded riots in the past year. My searches confirmed no ongoing unrest or emerging political crises as of mid-February 2026. For the first resolution date (Feb 22), 23 days of the 30-day window have already passed with no reported riots, making it mathematically nearly impossible to reach 11 riots in the remaining 7 days. For the second date (Mar 17), the window is mostly in the future, justifying a marginally higher probability, but reaching an unprecedented 11 riots in a single month remains extremely remote. Thus, I am applying the minimum allowed probability (0.05) and a marginally higher one for the later date (0.06) to strictly satisfy the requirements.
